headline: Procter And Gamble Taps Cynthia Rowley For Premium Downy Scent Launch
summary: Downy, a Procter & Gamble (NYSE:PG) brand, is launching Boutique Botanicals, a new line of in-wash scent bead boosters. The collection features nature-inspired fragrances created in collaboration with fashion designer Cynthia Rowley. The launch broadens Procter & Gamble’s premium home care range and introduces a cross-industry designer partnership into its fabric care portfolio. For Procter & Gamble, fabric and home care are core parts of the business, and fragrance has long been a key way...

headline: The Dividend Strategy That Beats the 4% Rule by $400,000 Over 20 Years on a $1 Million Portfolio
summary: A 65-year-old retiree with $1 million who follows the standard 4% rule withdraws $40,000 in the first year, then increases that amount over time to keep pace with inflation. A dividend-focused alternative starts slightly lower, at about $38,000 in annual income from a 3.8% blended yield, but does not require selling shares. Over 20 years, ... The Dividend Strategy That Beats the 4% Rule by $400,000 Over 20 Years on a $1 Million Portfolio

headline: New Downy Boutique Botanicals Scent Beads Bring Nature-Inspired Boutique Fragrances to Your Laundry
summary: CINCINNATI, June 09, 2026--Downy is launching Boutique Botanicals, a new line of in-wash scent bead boosters featuring nature-inspired boutique fragrances for your laundry. Crafted by the world-class perfumers at the Downy Fine Fragrance House, the team behind the cult-favorite Unlimited N.26, the collection includes three botanically-driven scents: Citrea, Peonia, and Folia.

headline: A Portfolio That Doubles Your Social Security Check—and Your Spouse’s
summary: A $1.2 million portfolio generating $7,200 per month produces $86,400 in annual income, equivalent to a blended yield of 7.2%. According to the Social Security Administration’s 2026 COLA fact sheet, the average aged couple receiving Social Security benefits collects about $3,208 per month, or $38,496 per year. That means a portfolio generating $7,200 per month ... A Portfolio That Doubles Your Social Security Check—and Your Spouse’s

headline: Get Paid Like an Indiana Police Officer With $5,000 a Month in Dividend Income After Taxes
summary: Five thousand dollars a month in spendable dividend income works out to $60,000 per year after federal tax, roughly equivalent to the salary of a typical police officer in Indiana. The headline yield shown on a brokerage statement does not tell the full story. Taxes, the type of distributions received, and future dividend growth all ... Get Paid Like an Indiana Police Officer With $5,000 a Month in Dividend Income After Taxes
